title: Gyrostachys longilabris
intro: Gyrostachys longilabris, also known as the Long-lipped Gyrostachys, is a species of flowering plant in the family Zingiberaceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential in treating various health conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Gyrostachys longilabris include gingerol, shogaol, and other phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Gyrostachys longilabris is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal remedies such as syrups and tinctures.
taxonomy: Gyrostachys longilabris is a species of plant in the family Acoraceae. It is a type of flowering plant, commonly known as a type of arum. The classification of Gyrostachys longilabris includes its genus, which is a part of the broader order Alismatales.
